text: The morning the river ran backward, no one in Hollow Mill noticed until the millwheel began to groan against its own turning. Old Pell, who had ground flour in that valley for forty years and buried two wives beside the water he trusted, stood in the doorway with a sack of barley on his shoulder and watched the current pull east toward the mountains it had spent a thousand years escaping. The fish came with it, silver and confused, swimming against an instinct older than the village itself. Pell set down the barley. He did not pray, because he had stopped believing anything was listening, but he counted the seconds it took the water to forget where the sea was, and when he reached forty he understood that something upstream had made a bargain, and that the bill, as bills always did, had come due downstream where the poor and the patient lived.
